name: Source Fidelity review for Ssr Assessment Stakes Boundaries Consequential Update bundle
summary: Accepted. The four newly staged sources have full-text access, source locators, and metadata consistent with the cited records. The New Mexico technical report, Wang et al., Rezayi et al., and Djagba et al. extracts preserve the reported sample sizes, metrics, and source-access boundaries without relying on abstract-only support.

findings.claim_or_issue: The New Mexico report states that validation sets are independent of training sets, but the extracted passage does not fully specify validation-set composition relative to approved synthetic responses.
findings.why_it_matters: This affects whether the source can support an independent validation claim stronger than controlled training augmentation.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged claim limited to training augmentation and retain the next action to check validation-set composition before making stronger validation language.

findings.claim_or_issue: Djagba et al. describe GPT-4 augmentation but one example label in the PDF appears to say GPT-5.
findings.why_it_matters: Unresolved model-version ambiguity should not be hidden in a method extract.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the artifact language that reports GPT-4 while explicitly flagging the apparent GPT-5 label for review.
